content_of_post_to_comment:
Hi,

I did always had the desire to do something one day, but the same desire wasn't there the next day. So I made an app that let people stick to their commitments and goals. Some of us have a will to do something, but as soon as the day comes to actually do it, the motivation is not there.

The idea of this app is based on the loss aversion, that the pain of losing something feels twice as powerful as the pleasure of an equal gain.

So the thought of losing money for not doing something you wanted to do the previous day is enough to make you go there and finish it.

That's how Commity works. A few dollars on the line does. Pin your location and select the radius. Select days and daily minutes. GPS confirms you are actually there and your money is yours, as long as you show up.

We all have bad days from time to time. Miss a day ? Commity let users win back two thirds of their original stake by finishing 5 sessions in the next 5 weeks or sooner.

I really hope this app can help some of you achieve your goals.

Thank you ver much for reading. You can find out more on our website. Thanks!
comment: The GPS/location verification angle is an interesting twist because it removes the usual "I'll do it later" loophole. I do wonder how people react when the goal is something less location-based, like writing or studying. The money stake might work well for habits with clear sessions, but the hardest part may be defining success without making the system easy to game.
